summary: Comprehensive diagnostic matrix for Linux kernel Hard LOCKUP scenarios
init_cmds: foreach UN bt | awk '/#[1-4] /{print $3,$5}' | paste - - - - | column -t

matrix.trigger: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu {cpu}
matrix.arg_hints: cpu: CPU number from lockup message
matrix.why: Examine what's running on the locked CPU
matrix.expect: Stack trace showing stuck function or spinlock

matrix.arg_hints: cpu: CPU number from lockup message
matrix.why: Examine what's running on the locked CPU
matrix.expect: Stack trace showing stuck function or spinlock

matrix.trigger: Kernel panic - not syncing: softlockup: hung tasks
matrix.why: List all tasks to identify hung processes
matrix.expect: Tasks in D (uninterruptible) or R (running) state

matrix.why: Examine tasks stuck in D state
matrix.expect: List of tasks in D state with memory addresses

matrix.trigger: Need to analyze uninterruptible tasks backtraces
matrix.action: foreach UN bt | awk '/#[1-4] /{print $3,$5}' | paste - - - - | column -t
matrix.why: Extract top frames from D state task backtraces
matrix.expect: Formatted top frames showing common stuck functions

matrix.arg_hints: addr: spinlock address from RDI register in backtrace
matrix.why: Check spinlock contention state and waiter information
matrix.expect: Spinlock head/tail values showing contention pattern

matrix.why: Examine task state, flags, and scheduling information
matrix.expect: Task state, flags, and pending signals

matrix.why: Check timer expiration and callback function
matrix.expect: Timer expiration time and callback function pointer

matrix.why: Examine ring buffer state and per-CPU pointers
matrix.expect: Ring buffer size, per-CPU buffer pointers

matrix.why: Check per-CPU ring buffer reader/writer positions
matrix.expect: Reader and writer positions, buffer state

matrix.why: Get kernel version, BIOS, and system configuration
matrix.expect: Kernel version, BIOS version, system uptime

matrix.trigger: Search for lockup-related messages in kernel log
matrix.why: Find lockup detection messages and RCU stall warnings
matrix.expect: Lockup messages with CPU numbers and timestamps

matrix.trigger: Need to examine backtrace of panicked task
matrix.why: Get stack trace of the currently selected task
matrix.expect: Complete stack trace with function addresses

matrix.trigger: Need to check backtrace for CPU {cpu}
matrix.arg_hints: cpu: CPU number from lockup or other task
matrix.why: Examine what was running on another CPU
matrix.expect: Stack trace showing other side of potential deadlock

matrix.arg_hints: modname: Module name from backtrace function symbols
matrix.why: Check module information and loaded addresses
matrix.expect: Module base address, size, and state

matrix.trigger: Need to examine memory at address {addr}
matrix.arg_hints: addr: Memory address from backtrace or structure, offset: 0x10 or 0x20
matrix.why: Dump raw memory contents for analysis
matrix.expect: Hex dump of memory contents

matrix.arg_hints: device: Device name from backtrace or task info
matrix.why: Check device driver and state information
matrix.expect: Device driver, major/minor numbers, state

matrix.arg_hints: addr: task_struct address from ps output for PID {pid}
matrix.why: Examine specific process state and resources
matrix.expect: Task state, flags, and resource usage

matrix.arg_hints: addr: Function address from backtrace minus {offset}
matrix.why: Disassemble function to examine code path
matrix.expect: Assembly instructions around the crash point

matrix.arg_hints: cpu: CPU number from lockup message
matrix.why: Examine register state at lockup time
matrix.expect: Register values including RIP, RSP, RDI
